# Product auto-generated code + category cascade — design

**Date:** 2026-07-14
**Features:** on `/product-management/product/new` (and edit):
1. **Auto-generated code** — make the product `code` field server-assigned (running number)
   instead of user-entered. Mirrors the shipped `2026-07-13-category-auto-code-design.md`.
2. **Cascade select** — replace the read-only Category / Sub Category display fields with a
   forced 3-level cascade: Category → Sub Category → Item Group.

## Problem

**Code:** On the Add Product form, `code` is a required free-text input (and it is editable on
the Edit form too). The backend now generates the code as a running number on create and owns
it thereafter. The UI must stop asking the user to type it, signal that the code is assigned
automatically, lock it in every mode, and stop sending a code on create.

**Category selection:** Today the user picks an Item Group directly (flat search across every
item group), and Category / Sub Category are read-only fields derived from the chosen item
group. Users want to narrow the choice top-down: pick a Category, which filters the Sub
Categories, which filters the Item Groups.

## Scope

- **In scope:** `ProductForm` (`pd-form.tsx`), the General tab (`pd-general-tab.tsx`), a new
  `components/lookup/lookup-sub-category.tsx`, the Zod schema and create DTO
  (`types/product.ts`), i18n cleanup, and new form/lookup tests.
- **Out of scope:** backend running-number generation (already implemented and confirmed
  ready — frontend-only change); any backend/payload change for the cascade (the product
  still persists only `product_item_group_id`; the backend derives category/sub-category);
  any other module's forms.

## Current state (as-is)

- `types/product.ts` — `createProductSchema.code` is
  `z.string().min(1, required).max(10, maxLength)` (required); `CreateProductDto.code: string`
  (required).
- `pd-general-tab.tsx` — code rendered as `<FieldLabel required>` + a branch:
  `isDisabled ? <ReadOnlyValue value={v.code}/> : <FieldInput maxLength={10}
  placeholder={t("codePlaceholder")} error=... {...form.register("code")} />`. Because
  `isDisabled = mode === "view" || isPending`, the code input is **editable in both add and
  edit** modes.
- `pd-form.tsx` — `buildPayload` always emits `code: values.code`; `onSubmit` add-branch
  calls `createProduct.mutate(payload)`.
- `code` is **not** part of `RequiredChecklist` (unchanged by this work).
- No test file exists under `routes/product-management/product/`.
- `field.autoGenerated` i18n key already exists (added by the category feature) —
  "Auto-generated" / "สร้างอัตโนมัติ".

## Design (to-be)

## Part 1 — Auto-generated code

Net rule (matches category): the product code is **never** an editable input. In add mode it
shows a disabled placeholder; in view/edit it shows the server-assigned value read-only. The
code label carries no `required` marker anywhere. A code is sent to the backend only on
update (unchanged), never on create.

### 1. Schema — `types/product.ts` `createProductSchema`

Relax `code` from required to optional (add submits no code; edit carries the existing
server value):

```ts
code: z.string().optional(),
```

### 2. DTO — `types/product.ts` `CreateProductDto`

```ts
code?: string;
```

### 3. Form field — `pd-general-tab.tsx`

Derive add mode locally (the tab does not receive `mode`, but `product` is undefined only in
add mode):

```ts
const isAdd = !product;
```

Replace the code `Field` so it never renders an editable input:

- `FieldLabel` for code drops the `required` prop.
- `isAdd` → a `disabled` `FieldInput` with `placeholder={tfl("autoGenerated")}`, no
  `form.register` binding (the value stays `""` in form state and is stripped on submit).
- otherwise (view/edit) → `<ReadOnlyValue value={v.code} />` (locked).
- Drop the code field's `data-invalid`, `error`, `maxLength`, and the
  `productManagement.product.codePlaceholder` reference — code can no longer be invalid.

### 4. Payload strip — `pd-form.tsx`

Add an `isAdd` parameter to `buildPayload` and omit the code on create:

```ts
const buildPayload = (values, product, dirtyFields, isAdd) => ({
  ...
  code: isAdd ? undefined : values.code,
  ...
});
```

`undefined` keys are dropped by `JSON.stringify` in `lib/http-client.ts`, so the create POST
carries no `code`. The update path passes `isAdd = false`, keeping the existing (locked)
value in the payload — identical to the category edit behavior. **Export `buildPayload`** so
it can be unit-tested as a pure function.

### 5. RequiredChecklist — `pd-required-checklist.tsx`

No change — `code` is not one of the checklist items.

### 6. i18n — `messages/{en,th}.json`

- Reuse the existing `field.autoGenerated` key — **no new key added**.
- Remove the now-unused `productManagement.product.codePlaceholder` from both `en.json` and
  `th.json`.

### 7. Tests — new `routes/product-management/product/pd-form.test.tsx`

Follow the existing form-test pattern in
`routes/product-management/category/category-form.test.tsx` (render inside the app's i18n +
QueryClient providers, drive with `@testing-library/react`).

Cases:
- **buildPayload (pure unit):** with `isAdd = true`, the returned payload has `code`
  absent/`undefined`; with `isAdd = false`, `payload.code` equals `values.code`.
- **Render — add mode:** the code input is `disabled`, shows the `autoGenerated` placeholder,
  and the code label renders no required asterisk.
- **Render — view mode:** given a `product` with a `code`, the code is shown read-only
  (as text, not an input).

## Part 2 — Category → Sub Category → Item Group cascade

Net rule: the product still persists only `product_item_group_id` (unchanged schema/DTO/
payload). Category and Sub Category are **transient UI state** used solely to narrow the Item
Group choice; the backend continues to derive them from the chosen item group. Selecting a
parent resets its descendants. On edit/view, all three prefill from the loaded product.

### 8. New lookup — `components/lookup/lookup-sub-category.tsx`

Mirror `lookup-item-group.tsx` exactly (lazy fetch, `none` button, badge = code, client-side
search):

- Props: `value`, `onValueChange(value, item?: SubCategoryDto)`, `disabled`, `placeholder`,
  `className`, `size`, `filterCategoryId?`, `defaultLabel`, `error`.
- Data: `useSubCategory({ perpage: -1 }, { enabled: hasOpened || !!value })`.
- Client-side filter: `is_active` **and** (`filterCategoryId`
  ? `sc.product_category_id === filterCategoryId` : keep) — `SubCategoryDto.product_category_id`
  is a direct field (mirrors how `LookupItemGroup` filters on `g.product_subcategory_id`).

`LookupCategory` (top-level, no parent filter) and `LookupItemGroup` (already has
`filterSubCategoryId`) already exist and are reused as-is.

### 9. Cascade wiring — `pd-general-tab.tsx`

- Add local state initialized from the loaded product:
  ```ts
  const [categoryId, setCategoryId] = useState(product?.product_category?.id ?? "");
  const [subCategoryId, setSubCategoryId] = useState(product?.product_sub_category?.id ?? "");
  ```
  (`categoryName` / `subCategoryName` local state already exists — keep for the read-only
  labels and view mode.)
- **Category field** (was `ReadOnlyValue`): render `LookupCategory` when editable, else
  `ReadOnlyValue value={categoryName}`. On change `(id, item?)`:
  `setCategoryId(id)`, `setCategoryName(item?.name ?? "")`, then **reset descendants** —
  `setSubCategoryId("")`, `setSubCategoryName("")`, and
  `form.setValue("product_item_group_id", "", { shouldDirty: true, shouldValidate: true })`.
  Pass `defaultLabel={product?.product_category?.name}`.
- **Sub Category field** (was `ReadOnlyValue`): render `LookupSubCategory`
  `filterCategoryId={categoryId}` `disabled={isDisabled || !categoryId}`, else `ReadOnlyValue`.
  On change `(id, item?)`: `setSubCategoryId(id)`, `setSubCategoryName(item?.name ?? "")`, and
  reset item group (`form.setValue("product_item_group_id", "", …)`). Pass
  `defaultLabel={product?.product_sub_category?.name}`.
- **Item Group field**: add `filterSubCategoryId={subCategoryId}` and
  `disabled`-when `!subCategoryId`. Keep the existing `handleItemGroupChange` side-effects
  (price/qty deviation, flags, tax profile). It may keep syncing `categoryName` /
  `subCategoryName` from the item's nested objects; that is redundant but harmless. The
  `required` marker on Item Group stays (it is the persisted required field).
- Category / Sub Category labels carry **no** `required` asterisk (they are UI gates, not
  schema fields). Item Group remains required.
- View mode: all three render as `ReadOnlyValue` (Category = `categoryName`, Sub Category =
  `subCategoryName`, Item Group = `product?.product_item_group?.name`) — same as today.

### 10. No schema / payload / checklist change

`product_item_group_id` stays the single required category-side field. `RequiredChecklist`,
`createProductSchema`, `CreateProductDto`, and `buildPayload` are untouched by the cascade.

### 11. Cascade tests

Add to the new test file (or a `lookup-sub-category.test.tsx`):
- **`LookupSubCategory` filter:** mock `useSubCategory` with sub-categories across two parent
  categories; render with `filterCategoryId` = one parent; open the popover and assert only
  that parent's sub-categories appear.
- **Gating:** in the General tab, the Sub Category lookup is `disabled` until a Category is
  selected, and the Item Group lookup is `disabled` until a Sub Category is selected.
- **Reset:** selecting/changing a Category clears the Item Group value
  (`product_item_group_id` becomes `""`).

## Data flow

```
User opens /product-management/product/new
  → ProductForm (mode="add"): GeneralTab code field disabled, placeholder "Auto-generated", no *
  → submit → buildPayload(..., isAdd=true) → code: undefined
  → createProduct.mutate(payload) → POST body without code
  → backend assigns running-number code → navigate to the new product
```

Edit: code shown read-only from `values.code`; update payload keeps it (`isAdd = false`).

Cascade:

```
Add mode
  → pick Category → Sub Category lookup enables, filtered to that category;
    Item Group cleared/disabled
  → pick Sub Category → Item Group lookup enables, filtered to that sub-category
  → pick Item Group → product_item_group_id set (+ deviation/flags/tax side-effects)
  → submit → payload carries product_item_group_id only (no category/sub ids)

Edit/View
  → categoryId / subCategoryId prefilled from product.product_category / product_sub_category
  → all three lookups show their current selection; changing a parent resets descendants
```

## Error handling / edge cases

- Schema no longer rejects an empty code, so add-mode validation passes with no code entered.
- Edit-mode code is present (from the loaded product), so the optional schema is satisfied.
- No change to backend error surfacing; existing mutation error toasts remain.
- Cascade: changing a Category with an Item Group already chosen clears
  `product_item_group_id`, so the form re-flags it as required (existing validation) until a
  new item group is picked — prevents a stale item group that no longer matches the category.
- Sub Category / Item Group lookups are disabled (not just empty) while their parent is
  unset, so the user cannot reach an inconsistent selection.

## Verification

- `bunx tsc --noEmit && bun test:run` clean.
- Manual (code): open `/product-management/product/new` — code field locked with
  "Auto-generated" placeholder, no asterisk; create succeeds and the new product shows a
  server-assigned code. Open an existing product and Edit — code shown, locked (read-only).
- Manual (cascade): on the Add form, Sub Category is disabled until a Category is picked and
  Item Group is disabled until a Sub Category is picked; each level lists only children of the
  chosen parent; changing the Category clears the Item Group. Open an existing product — all
  three prefill correctly; create/update persists the selected item group.
